channel.title: Piero Savastano
channel.description: Started as a young research scientist specialized on computational neuroscience, cognitive psychology, artificial life and artificial intelligence. Ended up creating videos, maintaining an AI open source framework and consulting internationally on AI.

item.title: Vibecodo e Vibegodo
item.description: A short, half-joking rant about open source, open weights, and the one freedom I refuse to give up.

item.title: How to Squeeze Money Out of a Vibecoder
item.description: The bottleneck has moved from writing code to securing it - and that shift is a business opportunity.

item.title: Already Out of Tokens?
item.description: Why usage of non-American models is exploding, what you actually save, and the catch nobody mentions.

item.title: AI Agents: Writing a Skill System From Scratch (Vibe-Coding the Cheshire Cat)
item.description: A hands-on vibe-coding session building an agent skill system from zero with the Cheshire Cat framework.

item.title: The Truth About Fable, After the Ban
item.description: The Mythos-to-Fable hype cycle, the ban, and the compute economics that quietly make these models dumber.

item.title: The Richard Dawkins Controversy
item.description: Dawkins says he thinks Claude is conscious. Everyone called him a senile old man. I think he nailed it.

item.title: Reading Brautigan's 'All Watched Over by Machines of Loving Grace'
item.description: Where the title of Amodei’s famous AI essay comes from - a 1967 Richard Brautigan poem, read aloud.

item.title: You Need a Passport to Use AI
item.description: ID and age checks are coming to AI, and OpenAI is turning into a kind of token bank you have to register with.

item.title: Which AI Do I Buy for My Company or Department?
item.description: AI is not a single product to buy in one block - it is separable pieces, and knowing them keeps you out of a vendor cage.

item.title: Cheshire Cat 2 Supports Skills
item.description: A 20-minute experiment giving the Cheshire Cat 2 framework agent skills - and the open questions about MCP and safety.

item.title: What's New in MCP
item.description: A new version of the Model Context Protocol lands July 28th: stateless transport, deprecations, tasks and MCP apps.

item.title: Work Shifts to the Bottlenecks
item.description: AI does not just automate tasks - it moves human work to whatever constraint is now scarce.

item.title: Is It Worth Writing Code?
item.description: A live conversation with Giuseppe Funicello on whether writing code, learning frameworks, and building products still pays off.

item.title: The Truth About the Fable Ban
item.description: Why the US government restricted Anthropic Fable and Mythos models, and why I think surveillance is the real story.

item.title: Live on RAI Radio1
item.description: I'm on Italian public radio talking Anthropic, the SpaceX valuation, and what AI means for programmers.

item.title: The Truth About Mythos and Fable 5
item.description: Why premium AI models are sold "by the sip" - and why the "too dangerous" safety framing is really about B2B pricing.

item.title: The 3 Loops of AI: Token, Tool, and Task
item.description: The word "loop" gets thrown around a lot in AI - but there are at least three nested ones, from single tokens up to whole tasks.

item.title: Fable 5 and Agents in a Loop
item.description: Fable 5 finally ships, and top agent builders now write loops instead of prompts - at a steep token cost.

item.title: MCP vs Skills, and Women in Tech
item.description: Which to cover next - MCP or agent skills - plus a hard, honest look at bringing more women into AI.

item.title: Anthropic: World Champions of Eloquent Gibberish
item.description: Anthropic keeps calling for an AI slowdown - but the simpler explanation is they fell behind on compute.

item.title: Mind and Brain Are the Same Thing
item.description: A neuroscientist pushes back on mind-body dualism: intelligence is physical, machines understand, and we are beautiful biological machines.

item.title: Help Pope Leo Smash the Data Centers
item.description: The new encyclical on AI is sharper and more political than I expected - and it's a little embarrassing that the Pope has to be the one saying it.

item.title: Vibe-Coding Session on the Pope's Encyclical: Highlights
item.description: Reading the new AI encyclical, building a Pope-vs-Big-Tech video game with Claude Code, and finding the exact limits of vibe coding.

item.title: Hello World
item.description: Inaugurating this new website
